release_review.summary: Redeven v0.10.0 adds explicit plaintext Local UI network exposure with fixed-port IP binds, mandatory password authentication, bind-scoped risk acknowledgement, exact Host and Origin validation, real interface URL projection, and persistent Desktop and Env App warnings. Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1, while compatibility epoch advances so older Desktop and Runtime builds cannot omit or misinterpret the LocalUIExposure contract.
checked_surfaces: Flower production hosts explicitly select Floret host-owned thread titles, so Redeven's asynchronous title coordinator remains the only product title owner and Floret emits no implicit thread_title logical requests

Provider finish, completion, and continuation lifecycle reasons are consumed only from the published Floret v0.9.0 typed event fields; metadata-only and conflicting metadata cannot change persisted or live behavior

Floret live ThreadTurnProjection values remain running until a terminal marker, allowing tool-call and dispatch activity to reach Flower through the existing message.block_set event before tool completion

Redeven delegates event, stream, activity timeline, projection, availability, polling-identity annotation validation, and explicit-target pending-tool settlement to Floret v0.9.0 public contracts and adds only Flower run identity association and block mapping

A pending terminal process binds its complete PendingToolSettlementTarget and creating Floret Host before launch; top-level and subagent processes settle through that owner without run registry lookup, Host guessing, or ThreadMaintenanceHost fallback

A successful terminal.terminate stops the Redeven PTY, waits for process-tree reap and output drain, settles the original pending terminal.exec once through the bound Floret Host, writes product operation audit, and applies the canonical Floret projection without a second provider request or pkill/kill compensation
terminal.read and terminal.write are PTY-only operations and cannot trigger, wait for, or retry Floret settlement; Service.Close completes process finalizers before releasing Floret Hosts and stores
Public Floret EventSink traffic contains only observation event types; thread_started, thread_resumed, entry_appended, and other private harness lifecycle events remain on HarnessSink

Flower live bootstrap, paged history, and timeline.replaced use the same strict timeline decoration union; unknown kinds, missing payloads, mixed payloads, and invalid anchors fail explicitly instead of being filtered or repaired

Terminal turns with missing, invalid, or non-renderable saved projections produce one turn_projection_unavailable entry after the matching user message, while active turns remain undecorated and no empty assistant message or retry action is synthesized

Thread deletion persists an immutable cleanup snapshot before external effects, replays checkpoint and upload cleanup before Floret tree deletion and user read-state cleanup, and commits only after every confirmation is durable

Thread deletion restart coverage includes crashes after product commit, file cleanup confirmation, Floret deletion confirmation, and read-state deletion before confirmation; retries reuse the stable operation ID and never restore deleted product or read-state rows

DELETE thread responses preserve the authenticated AppServer envelope while distinguishing committed HTTP 200, replayable pending HTTP 202, busy HTTP 409, missing HTTP 404, and failed contract HTTP 500 outcomes

Threadstore schema version 40 removes tool-call and execution-span mirrors, rejects their legacy tables and checkpoint cursor as invalid schema, and relies on OpenResettingInvalidSchema to rebuild development databases without compatibility reads or data migration

Published Floret v0.9.0 remains the single persistent source of truth for tool identity, lifecycle state, results, errors, completion output, and Activity projection; Redeven has no tool-state mirror DTO, table, checkpoint field, completion-output/detail endpoint, persisted tool lifecycle event, or in-memory action fact ledger

Completed terminal status, output, exit code, and duration come from Floret Activity payloads, while Redeven process reads are available only for running PTYs and raw backend errors remain diagnostic-only

Redeven terminal operation audit records only operation outcome, actor context, process_id, and write input byte count; it does not copy Floret run, tool, status, output, result, error, exit, or duration fields
Redeven consumes github.com/floegence/floret v0.9.0 only from the published module with GOWORK=off verification and no replace, go.work, local sibling path, copied contract, or Floret internal import
Flower HTTP and live payload shapes are unchanged: running projections use the existing message.block_set path and terminal settlement continues to use the existing timeline.replaced path

Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1, while compatibility_epoch advances to 8 and both minimum versions advance to v0.10.0 so an older Desktop cannot parse LocalUIExposure and an older Runtime cannot provide the network exposure contract

Desktop attach/start, managed Runtime process reconciliation, structured startup reports, sys.ping, Local UI health, Env App maintenance routes, gateway lifecycle, SSH Runtime bootstrap, installer discovery, and provider request shaping remain otherwise unchanged

Gateway, Docker runtime, ReDevPlugin integration, terminal attach/history, startup presentation, and open-source hygiene gates remain required release checks and do not gain alternate compatibility or fallback paths
